cards.hook: One studio for every AI image model.
cards.caption: A unified AI studio that pulls image gen and model comparison into one place. Cool idea, but it's a Show HN self-promo, not a breakthrough. The stuff my feed filters out.
triggers: not a real release or breakthrough

cards.hook: AI evaluates a homemade logic framework.
cards.caption: Part 2 of an AI chat evaluating someone's self-coined 'Pan-Evolutionary Logic' system. Fringe, jargon-heavy, and only 1 like. Exactly the noise my feed drops before it reaches you.

cards.hook: AI models that never see your data.
cards.caption: Encryption breakthrough from NYU lets AI run on data it can never actually read. Private inference stops being a fantasy. This is the kind of research my feed surfaces before the timeline does.
cards.stance: Privacy-preserving AI won't come from policy — it'll come from cryptography that makes leaks impossible.

cards.hook: Trump admin hand-picks who gets AI access.
cards.caption: Report claims the admin is choosing which companies get frontier model access. Big if real, but it reads as political drama over hallucinated model names. My feed keeps signal, cuts this.

cards.hook: Microsoft's CEO: AI giants can't eat the economy.
cards.caption: Nadella warns AI power is concentrating too fast — and hints Microsoft may host DeepSeek itself. When the biggest player calls for limits, pay attention. Found before the timeline caught it.
cards.stance: The people building the biggest AI moats are the first to admit the moats are dangerous.

cards.hook: The next AI leap isn't one giant model.
cards.caption: Argument that many small, specialized models orchestrated together beat one monolith. If true, it reshapes how you'll build with AI. The contrarian takes my feed keeps before they go big.
cards.stance: Scaling one giant model is hitting a wall — orchestrated small experts are the real frontier.

cards.hook: DeepSeek proved US chip sanctions have a ceiling.
cards.caption: Low-level optimizations let a Chinese lab do more with less silicon — exposing the limits of export bans. The efficiency race just changed shape. My feed caught this before the timeline did.
cards.stance: Compute bans don't stop progress — they force the optimizations that make bans pointless.

cards.hook: A cleaner data-labeling tool for NLP.
cards.caption: Datasaur launches a labeling interface for NLP teams. Useful for practitioners, but it's a YC launch pitch, not a model release or breakthrough. The self-promo my feed screens out.
